summary: Determine whether Tenstorrent loads all MoE experts resident or supports dynamic/streamed loading (GPT-OSS reference)
logs: Investigating TT MoE expert residency vs dynamic loading across 3 angles

investigation.area: GPT-OSS MoE expert weight placement on Tenstorrent (tt-metal models/demos/gpt_oss)
investigation.mechanism: No host-streaming / on-demand / prefetch-from-host expert path exists. The only caching is an OPTIONAL on-disk tensor cache (cache_file_name -> `<name>_dtype_<dtype>_layout_<layout>.tensorbin`): if the tilized/sharded tensor was serialized to disk on a prior run, `ttnn.as_tensor(...)` loads it directly into device DRAM at init (skipping the torch state_dict prep), otherwise it tilizes from the torch state_dict and writes the cache. This is a build-time disk cache loaded ONCE into DRAM at init, not per-token/per-layer host streaming. There is no analog to llama.cpp `--cpu-moe` / `-ot` host-resident expert offload. Memory placement is `ttnn.DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG` (interleaved DRAM) for the low-latency `experts/` path and `MemoryConfig(HEIGHT_SHARDED, BufferType.DRAM, ...)` for the throughput fused `moe_gpt` path. Distribution: expert-parallel (EP) across the mesh ep_axis combined with tensor-parallel (TP) sharding of the intermediate/hidden dims. mesh_4x8() (Galaxy/TG, 32 devices) = decode TP=8, EP=4; the throughput path shards experts so each device owns `num_experts_per_device = num_experts/num_devices` complete experts (128/32 = 4 experts/device for 120B; 32/32 = 1 for 20B). Default expert weight dtype is `ttnn.bfloat4_b` (4-bit block float, the on-device representation of the MXFP4 source weights); biases are `ttnn.bfloat16`.
investigation.findings:
## Summary
For GPT-OSS on Tenstorrent (tt-metal `models/demos/gpt_oss/`), **ALL routed experts are loaded resident into device DRAM at model init**. There is NO dynamic / on-demand / per-token / host-streamed expert loading. The forward (decode/prefill) path consumes already-resident DRAM weight tensors with zero host-to-device transfers per token.

## (a) Loaded at init into DRAM, never streamed
Expert weights are materialized once during `__init__` via `ttnn.as_tensor(...)` with `device=mesh_device` and `memory_config=ttnn.DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG`. The tensor passed in covers the FULL expert dimension (`config.num_experts`), so every expert is placed on device.

`models/demos/gpt_oss/tt/experts/weights.py` (`load_expert_weights`):
```python
gate_proj_tt = ttnn.as_tensor(
    gate_proj,
    device=mesh_device,
    layout=ttnn.TILE_LAYOUT,
    dtype=weight_dtype,
    mesh_mapper=col_mesh_mapper,
    cache_file_name=get_cache_file_name(tensor_cache_path, "gate_proj"),
    memory_config=ttnn.DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG,
)
```
(identical `memory_config=ttnn.DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG` for `up_proj`, `down_proj`, and all biases). The shapes reshape to `(1, config.num_experts, hidden, intermediate)` — i.e. all experts.

Model build is one-shot at init: `model.py` builds `self.layers = [DecoderLayer(..., substate(state_dict, f"model.layers.{layer_idx}"), ...) for layer_idx in range(num_hidden_layers)]`; weights "remain on device between forward calls ... no dynamic loading/unloading mechanisms". Decode forward (`experts/decode.py`) does `ttnn.sparse_matmul(hidden_states, weights.gate_proj, ...)` etc. — reads resident device tensors, **no `ttnn.from_torch` / `as_tensor` / `to_device` / `copy_host_to_device` in the forward path**.

## (b) dtype & mesh distribution
- Default expert dtype `weight_dtype=ttnn.bfloat4_b` (4-bit; on-device form of the MXFP4 source); biases `ttnn.bfloat16`. Confirmed in `experts/weights.py`, `experts_throughput/weights.py`, README usage example, and mlp.py call sites (`weight_dtype=ttnn.bfloat4_b`).
- Two expert implementations:
  - Low-latency `experts/`: experts replicated/sharded by TP. Mesh mappers `column_parallel` (shard dim -1) and `row_parallel` (shard dim -2) shard the intermediate/hidden dims across the TP axis; the expert dimension itself is carried in the tensor.
  - Throughput `experts_throughput/` (Galaxy/TG, disabled on Blackhole): true expert-parallel — `_shard_experts_by_device` uses `ttnn.ShardTensorToMesh(mesh_device, dim=shard_dim)` to give "each device a subset of experts (num_experts / num_devices experts per device)". Fused `moe_gpt` path places weights `HEIGHT_SHARDED` in DRAM and uses `ShardTensor2dMesh(dims=(0,1))` over (ring_devices, mesh_cols) for combined EP×TP.
- `MeshConfig` (config.py): `mesh_4x8()` => decode TP=8, EP=4, DP=1; prefill TP=8, SP=4, EP=1. `mesh_1x8()` (LoudBox, single 8-device row) => TP=8, EP=1. ep_axis = the non-TP axis.

## (c) Host-resident / streaming / prefetch path
None for experts. The grep for stream/prefetch/on_demand/offload was rate-limited, but: every expert `ttnn.as_tensor`/`from_torch` uses `device=mesh_device` + DRAM memory_config at init; forward path has no host transfer; `get_cache_file_name` only builds a `.tensorbin` disk-cache path. WebFetch of README confirms no streaming/offload mentioned. The disk cache is the closest thing to "prefetch" but it loads into DRAM once at init.

## (d) 120B params vs device DRAM (cross-check tt-inference-server release_model_spec.json)
GPT-OSS-120B: 128 experts, MXFP4 routed-expert weights ≈ ~60 GiB at 4-bit (~117B of the 120B params are in the experts). All of it must sit resident in device DRAM — distributed across the mesh (TG/Galaxy 32 chips: 4 experts/device; T3K 8 chips). It is NOT held in host RAM during inference.
release_model_spec.json (tt-inference-server):
- `id_gpt-oss_gpt-oss-120b_t3k` Device `"T3K"`, host `"min_ram_gb": 300.0`, `"min_disk_gb": 260`, `"param_count": 120`.
- `id_gpt-oss_gpt-oss-120b_galaxy` Device `"GALAXY"`, `"max_concurrency": 32`, host `"min_ram_gb": 300.0`, `"min_disk_gb": 260`, `"data_parallel_size": 4`.
- 20B: host `"min_ram_gb": 50.0`, `"min_disk_gb": 60` on T3K/GALAXY_T3K/GALAXY.
The large host `min_ram_gb` (300 GB for 120B) is for LOADING/converting the full-precision HF checkpoint on the host before tilizing to device DRAM — it is a load-time host requirement, not evidence of host-resident experts during inference. Device DRAM must hold all expert weights (sharded EP×TP across the mesh).

keyFacts.claim: Every expert weight tensor is placed in device DRAM at init via ttnn.as_tensor with DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG; tensor spans all num_experts
keyFacts.evidence: experts/weights.py: gate_proj_tt = ttnn.as_tensor(gate_proj, device=mesh_device, layout=ttnn.TILE_LAYOUT, dtype=weight_dtype, mesh_mapper=col_mesh_mapper, cache_file_name=get_cache_file_name(tensor_cache_path, "gate_proj"), memory_config=ttnn.DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG,) ; gate_proj = state_dict["gate_up_proj"][..., ::2].reshape(1, config.num_experts, config.hidden_size, config.intermediate_size)

keyFacts.claim: Forward (decode) path reads resident device weight tensors; no per-token host-to-device transfer
keyFacts.evidence: experts/decode.py: gate = ttnn.sparse_matmul(hidden_states, weights.gate_proj, ...); down = ttnn.sparse_matmul(down_input, weights.down_proj, ...) — zero ttnn.from_torch/as_tensor/to_device/copy_host_to_device in the forward function

keyFacts.claim: Throughput path is expert-parallel: each device holds a subset (num_experts/num_devices) of complete experts, sharded across the mesh, in DRAM
keyFacts.evidence: experts_throughput/weights.py _shard_experts_by_device: ttnn.as_tensor(weights, device=mesh_device, ..., mesh_mapper=ttnn.ShardTensorToMesh(mesh_device, dim=shard_dim), ..., memory_config=ttnn.DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG) with docstring 'Each device gets a subset of experts (num_experts / num_devices experts per device).'

keyFacts.claim: Expert weight dtype defaults to 4-bit bfloat4_b (on-device form of MXFP4 source); biases bfloat16
keyFacts.evidence: experts/weights.py signature: weight_dtype=ttnn.bfloat4_b; experts_throughput/weights.py: weight_dtype: ttnn.DataType = ttnn.bfloat4_b; mlp.py: load_throughput_expert_weights(..., weight_dtype=ttnn.bfloat4_b, ...) and Experts(..., weight_dtype=ttnn.bfloat4_b, ...)

keyFacts.claim: Expert distribution across mesh is EP x TP; Galaxy 4x8 = decode TP=8, EP=4 (=> 4 experts/device for 128 experts)
keyFacts.evidence: config.py mesh_4x8(): 'decode: TP=8, EP=4, DP=1; prefill: TP=8, SP=4, EP=1, DP=4' return MeshConfig((4, 8), decode=ModeConfig(tp=8, ep=4)); throughput config docstring: 'expert distribution across 32 Galaxy devices (4 experts per device)'

keyFacts.claim: The only caching is an on-disk tilized-tensor cache loaded into DRAM at init; not host streaming during inference
keyFacts.evidence: general_utils.py: def get_cache_file_name(tensor_cache_path, name): return f"{tensor_cache_path}/{name}" if tensor_cache_path else None ; experts_throughput/weights.py: 'If the cached fused weights for this layer already exist on disk, ttnn.as_tensor below will load them via load_tensor_flatbuffer' -> *.tensorbin

keyFacts.claim: No streaming/prefetch/on_demand/offload symbols in expert loading; weights stay resident between forward calls
keyFacts.evidence: model.py analysis: 'Once allocated during __init__, all weights remain on device between forward calls. The code shows no dynamic loading/unloading mechanisms' ; mlp.py/decode.py contain no stream/prefetch/on_demand/offload keywords

keyFacts.claim: tt-inference-server release_model_spec.json: gpt-oss-120b needs host min_ram_gb 300 (load-time), disk 260, runs on T3K and GALAXY; 20B needs host 50GB
keyFacts.evidence: id_gpt-oss_gpt-oss-120b_t3k Device 'T3K' 'min_ram_gb': 300.0 'min_disk_gb': 260 'param_count': 120 ; id_gpt-oss_gpt-oss-120b_galaxy Device 'GALAXY' max_concurrency 32 'min_ram_gb': 300.0 data_parallel_size 4 ; gpt-oss-20b 'min_ram_gb': 50.0 'min_disk_gb': 60

keyFacts.claim: Throughput expert path (all_to_all dispatch/moe_gpt) is unsupported on Blackhole; only batch=1 low-latency DRAM-resident expert path runs there
keyFacts.evidence: general_utils.py throughput_experts_supported_on_arch(): 'Currently disabled on Blackhole; only the batch=1 low-latency expert path runs there.' return not is_blackhole()

keyFacts.claim: 120B host min_ram_gb (300GB) is for loading/converting the HF checkpoint on host, not for holding experts in host RAM during inference; device DRAM holds all experts
keyFacts.evidence: create_fused_moe_gpt_config comment: 'Skip the expensive state_dict prep (bfloat16->float32 expansion + per-device permute/cat across 32 chips x 128 experts)' — host work is one-time prep before DRAM placement; forward path has no host access

corrections.shouldBe: In the CURRENT spec (release_version 0.15.0), the gpt-oss-120b GALAXY entry has max_concurrency=1 and data_parallel_size=4 (not max_concurrency=32). The 32 figure the investigation cited is stale / was a different field. All OTHER spec values are confirmed verbatim: 120b T3K param_count=120, min_disk_gb=260, min_ram_gb=300.0; 120b GALAXY data_parallel_size=4, min_ram_gb=300.0, min_disk_gb=260; 20b min_ram_gb=50.0, min_disk_gb=60 on T3K/GALAXY_T3K/GALAXY. This is a metric drift that does NOT affect the load-bearing residency conclusion.

corrections.claim: release_model_spec.json keyed by flat ids like id_gpt-oss_gpt-oss-120b_t3k with top-level Device/min_ram_gb fields
corrections.shouldBe: The spec schema has changed: it is now nested as model_specs["openai/gpt-oss-120b"][<DEVICE>]["vLLM"]["gpt_oss"] with model_id="id_gpt-oss_gpt-oss-120b_t3k" and the min_ram_gb/min_disk_gb/param_count fields living under device_model_spec. The model_id strings the investigation cited still exist; only the structural location changed. Does not affect conclusions.

confirmed: residentAllExperts = yes-all-resident is CORRECT. experts/weights.py load_expert_weights places all six expert tensors (gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj + 3 biases) via ttnn.as_tensor with device=mesh_device and memory_config=ttnn.DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG; the torch tensors are reshaped to (1, config.num_experts, hidden, intermediate) i.e. the FULL expert dimension. Verified verbatim.

dynamicLoadingAvailable = no is CORRECT. There is NO host-streaming / on-demand / prefetch / offload path for expert weights anywhere in models/demos/gpt_oss. Searched mlp.py, model.py, experts/{decode,prefill,operations,__init__}.py, experts_throughput/{decode,fused_decode,weights}.py for stream/prefetch/offload/on_demand/cpu_moe/swap/paging/reload — only non-weight hits: a 'Streamlined MoE MLP' docstring, 'stream-concatenate' comments referring to ACTIVATION tiling to reduce peak DRAM, and a combine-writer code comment. No analog to llama.cpp --cpu-moe / -ot exists.

Forward (decode) path reads resident device tensors with no per-token host transfer. experts/decode.py decode_forward uses only ttnn.sparse_matmul on weights.gate_proj / weights.up_proj / weights.down_proj (already-resident ttnn.Tensor fields) plus ttnn.add/mul/sum/permute; contains zero ttnn.from_torch / as_tensor / to_device / copy_host_to_device. Prefill path (experts/prefill.py) is identical in this respect.

Model build is one-shot at init. model.py: self.layers = [DecoderLayer(...) for layer_idx in range(hf_config.num_hidden_layers)]. The copy_host_to_device calls in model.py are all for activations/tokens/inputs/trace replay, NOT expert weights. layer.py builds self.mlp = MLP(...) once; mlp.py __init__ constructs Experts/ThroughputExperts once from experts_state_dict.

Throughput path is genuine expert-parallel, all DRAM-resident. experts_throughput/weights.py _shard_experts_by_device uses mesh_mapper=ttnn.ShardTensorToMesh(mesh_device, dim=shard_dim) with memory_config=ttnn.DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG; docstring verbatim: 'Each device gets a subset of experts (num_experts / num_devices experts per device).' Fused moe_gpt weights use MemoryConfig(TensorMemoryLayout.HEIGHT_SHARDED, BufferType.DRAM, ...) and ShardTensor2dMesh(dims=(0,1), mesh_shape=(ring_devices, mesh_cols)) for combined EP x TP. experts_per_device computed as 1 (20b) or 4 (120b) per the in-code comments.

Expert dtype defaults to 4-bit bfloat4_b; biases bfloat16. experts/weights.py signature weight_dtype=ttnn.bfloat4_b and bias_dtype = ttnn.bfloat16; experts_throughput/weights.py weight_dtype: ttnn.DataType = ttnn.bfloat4_b; mlp.py passes weight_dtype=ttnn.bfloat4_b at all three call sites (load_throughput_expert_weights, ThroughputExperts, Experts). Verified verbatim.

Mesh distribution EP x TP confirmed. config.py mesh_4x8() returns MeshConfig((4, 8), decode=ModeConfig(tp=8, ep=4)); mesh_1x8() returns MeshConfig((1, 8), decode=ModeConfig(tp=8, ep=1)). MeshConfig sets ep_axis = 0 if tp_axis==1 else 1 and prefill defaults to sp=mesh_shape[0], ep=1.

Only caching is an on-disk tilized-tensor cache loaded into DRAM once at init. general_utils.py: get_cache_file_name(tensor_cache_path, name) returns f'{tensor_cache_path}/{name}' if tensor_cache_path else None. experts_throughput/weights.py uses cache filename format f'{cache_base}_dtype_{dtype.name}_layout_{layout.name}.tensorbin' loaded via ttnn.as_tensor/load_tensor_flatbuffer; comment confirms it skips the expensive state_dict prep on warm cache. This is a build-time disk cache, NOT per-token host streaming.

Throughput path disabled on Blackhole. general_utils.py throughput_experts_supported_on_arch() returns not is_blackhole() with docstring 'Currently disabled on Blackhole; only the batch=1 low-latency expert path runs there.' Verified verbatim.

Host min_ram_gb (300GB for 120b) is a load-time host requirement for converting the HF checkpoint, not evidence of host-resident experts during inference. Confirmed by create_fused_moe_gpt config comment that the state_dict prep (bfloat16->float32 expansion + per-device permute/cat across 32 chips x 128 experts) is one-time work skipped on warm cache, and by the absence of any host weight access in the forward path.
verification.summary: VERIFIED against tt-metal@main and tt-inference-server@main. The two load-bearing conclusions are CORRECT: residentAllExperts = yes-all-resident, and dynamicLoadingAvailable = no. All GPT-OSS routed experts are materialized once at __init__ via ttnn.as_tensor with memory_config=ttnn.DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG, spanning the full config.num_experts dimension, and remain resident in device DRAM (sharded EP x TP across the mesh). The decode and prefill forward paths read only already-resident ttnn.Tensor weight fields via ttnn.sparse_matmul and contain zero per-token host->device weight transfers (no from_torch/as_tensor/to_device/copy_host_to_device on weights). An exhaustive keyword search across the gpt_oss tree (stream/prefetch/offload/on_demand/cpu_moe/swap/paging) found NO host-streaming or on-demand expert mechanism; the only 'stream' references are an MLP docstring and activation 'stream-concatenate' comments (peak-DRAM reduction for activations, not weights). There is no analog to llama.cpp --cpu-moe / -ot host-resident expert offload. The only weight cache is a build-time on-disk .tensorbin (format _dtype_{dtype.name}_layout_{layout.name}.tensorbin) loaded once into DRAM at init. dtype defaults bfloat4_b (4-bit, MXFP4-derived) with bfloat16 biases; mesh_4x8() = decode TP=8/EP=4, mesh_1x8() = TP=8/EP=1; throughput path is true expert-parallel (num_experts/num_devices complete experts per device, HEIGHT_SHARDED in DRAM) and is disabled on Blackhole. Two MINOR corrections, neither affecting the residency conclusion: (1) the current release_model_spec.json (release_version 0.15.0) shows gpt-oss-120b GALAXY with max_concurrency=1 and data_parallel_size=4, NOT max_concurrency=32 as the investigation stated; (2) the spec is now nested under model_specs[hf_repo][DEVICE][engine][impl] rather than flat top-level ids, though the cited model_id strings and the min_ram_gb=300/min_disk_gb=260/param_count=120 (120b) and min_ram_gb=50/min_disk_gb=60 (20b) values are all confirmed verbatim. Key file paths: /models/demos/gpt_oss/tt/experts/weights.py, /tt/experts/decode.py, /tt/experts/prefill.py, /tt/experts_throughput/weights.py, /tt/mlp.py, /tt/model.py, /tt/layer.py, /config.py, /utils/general_utils.py (all under github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/blob/main).

investigation.area: Generic MoE expert residency in tt_transformers (Qwen3-MoE / Qwen3-Next class) and the shared models/common MoE module
investigation.findings:
## Summary

In tt-metal / tt_transformers there is **no dynamic / on-demand / host-streamed expert loading** (nothing analogous to llama.cpp `--cpu-moe`). Every code path I found uploads **all** expert weights to device DRAM at model-load time via `ttnn.as_tensor(...)` / `ttnn.from_torch(...)` and keeps them resident for the life of the run. The only "host" copy is the transient torch `state_dict` consumed during init; once uploaded it is not used per-step.

## 1. tt_transformers MoE = Mixtral only, all experts resident

The decoder selects MoE vs dense MLP from a single flag (`models/tt_transformers/tt/decoder.py`):
```python
if getattr(self.args, "is_mixture_of_experts", False):
    self.feed_forward = TtMoeLayer(...)   # with TtMixtralMLP experts
else:
    self.feed_forward = MLP(...)
```

`is_mixture_of_experts` and the expert count are detected purely from Mixtral's `block_sparse_moe` key naming in the HF state_dict (`models/tt_transformers/tt/model_config.py`, lines 3069 / 3103-3105):
```python
self.is_mixture_of_experts = any([".experts." in k for k in state_dict.keys()])
...
if getattr(self, "is_mixture_of_experts", False):
    self.moe = True
    self.num_experts = max([int(item[-11]) + 1 for item in keys_dict if "block_sparse_moe.experts" in item])
```

The actual expert weights are loaded in `models/tt_transformers/tt/mixtral_mlp.py` (`TtMixtralMLP.__init__`). **All 8 experts are concatenated and uploaded to DRAM at construction**, sharded across the 8-device mesh (one expert per device), never streamed:
```python
torch_weight = lambda name: torch.concat(
    [ self.state_dict[f"{base_name(expert_num)}.{name}.weight"].permute(1, 0)...
      for expert_num in range(8) ], dim=0)
...
as_tensor = lambda name: ttnn.as_tensor(
    torch_weight(name),
    dtype=dtypes[name],
    device=self.mesh_device,
    mesh_mapper=ttnn.ShardTensorToMesh(self.mesh_device, dim=0),
    layout=self.model_config["MLP_W_LAYOUT_TILE"],
    memory_config=self.get_mem_config(name, torch_weight),   # create_dram_sharded_mem_config(...)
    cache_file_name=cache_name(name),
)
self.w1 = as_tensor("w1"); self.w2 = as_tensor("w2"); self.w3 = as_tensor("w3")
```
`get_mem_config` returns `create_dram_sharded_mem_config(...)`, i.e. weights live in device **DRAM**. The gate is likewise uploaded once in `mixtral_moe.py` (`TtMoeLayer.__init__`, `self.gates_H8 = ttnn.as_tensor(... device=self.mesh_device ...)`). In `forward()` the experts (`expert_i_HH = self.experts`) are simply *called* — `weights = expert_i_HH(input_i_1SBH, mode=mode)` — with no weight load/move. `cache_file_name` is only an on-disk cache of the *pre-formatted device tensor* to speed re-init; it is not per-step host streaming.

## 2. Qwen3-Next / Qwen3-MoE in tt_transformers: NOT supported there

`model_config.py`'s `LOCAL_HF_PARAMS` lists only dense Qwen3 (`"Qwen3-32B"`, `"Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct"`) and `model_params/` only has `Qwen3-32B` / `Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct` (both dense). The MoE detection (`int(item[-11])`, `block_sparse_moe.experts`) is hard-coded to **Mixtral key naming**, so a Qwen3-MoE checkpoint would not be handled by the tt_transformers MoE path. Code searches for `Qwen3Next`, `Qwen3-Next`, `Qwen3-80B`, `Qwen3MoeForCausalLM`, and `A3B` returned **no results** in the repo (as of main, June 2026). So Qwen3-Next-80B-A3B is not supported in tt_transformers.

## 3. The generic configurable MoE module (covers Qwen3-235B class) — also fully resident

A separate, newer module `models/common/modules/moe/tt_moe_decode.py` (`TTMoEDecode`) is the generic MoE decode block driven by per-model YAMLs in `models/common/modules/moe/configs/` (includes `qwen3_235b.yaml`, `qwen35_35b.yaml`, `qwen35_397b.yaml`, deepseek_v3, gpt_oss, etc.). This is the path for large routed-expert models. It is **also entirely resident**:

- `_TTMoEDecodeExpertState.__init__` takes host torch weight tensors `[L, num_routed_experts, H, N]` and uploads **all routed experts** sharded across the mesh on the expert dim:
```python
def _shard_experts(t):
    return ttnn.from_torch(t, device=mesh_device, layout=ttnn.ROW_MAJOR_LAYOUT,
        dtype=ttnn.bfloat16, mesh_mapper=ttnn.ShardTensorToMesh(mesh_device, dim=1))
tt_w0 = _shard_experts(mapped_torch_w0); tt_w1 = ...; tt_w2 = ...
```
- They are then packed and **quantized to `bfloat4_b` and placed on DRAM-sharded mem configs** (`get_weight_mem_configs` + `quantize_weights_via_host`), stored as `self.tt_w0_w1`, `self.tt_w2`. `forward()` consumes these resident tensors via `ttnn.experimental.moe_compute(... self.expert_state.tt_w0_w1, self.expert_state.tt_w2 ...)`. Module docstring: *"Weights upload as bfloat4_b ... routed experts are sharded across the dispatch axis; shared experts are replicated."*
- The expert *mapping* (`all_to_all_dispatch_metadata`) only selects which **already-resident, on-device** expert each token routes to (cross-device token dispatch on the mesh), NOT which weights to fetch from host.

## 4. Explicit confirmation that path/streamed loading does NOT exist

`_TTMoEDecodeExpertState._load_weights` is an unimplemented stub:
```python
def _load_weights(self):
    # TODO (AM) eventually support loading weights from path
    pass
```
This is the only hook for any non-resident loading, and it is a no-op TODO. There is no host-paging, prefetch-from-host, or per-step expert fetch anywhere in either the tt_transformers Mixtral path or the generic `TTMoEDecode` path.

## Memory-sizing implication
For VRAM/DRAM sizing: **count 100% of expert weights as device-resident**. tt_transformers Mixtral shards the 8 experts one-per-device across an 8-chip mesh (per-device DRAM holds total_expert_bytes / num_devices). The generic module shards `num_routed_experts` across the mesh and stores them quantized (bf4 for routed/shared expert matmuls). No fraction of experts can be left on host at runtime.

keyFacts.claim: tt_transformers detects MoE solely from Mixtral-style state_dict keys; flag drives module choice
keyFacts.evidence: model_config.py:3069 self.is_mixture_of_experts = any([".experts." in k for k in state_dict.keys()]) ; 3105 self.num_experts = max([int(item[-11]) + 1 for item in keys_dict if "block_sparse_moe.experts" in item])

keyFacts.claim: Decoder instantiates TtMoeLayer (Mixtral) when is_mixture_of_experts else dense MLP
keyFacts.evidence: decoder.py: if getattr(self.args, "is_mixture_of_experts", False): self.feed_forward = TtMoeLayer(...) else: self.feed_forward = MLP(...)

keyFacts.claim: All 8 Mixtral experts are concatenated and uploaded to device DRAM at init, sharded one-per-device; never streamed
keyFacts.evidence: mixtral_mlp.py: torch_weight = lambda name: torch.concat([...for expert_num in range(8)], dim=0); as_tensor = lambda name: ttnn.as_tensor(torch_weight(name), device=self.mesh_device, mesh_mapper=ttnn.ShardTensorToMesh(self.mesh_device, dim=0), memory_config=self.get_mem_config(name, torch_weight), ...); self.w1 = as_tensor("w1")

keyFacts.claim: Mixtral expert weights placed in DRAM-sharded memory (create_dram_sharded_mem_config)

keyFacts.claim: Qwen3-Next / Qwen3-80B-A3B / Qwen3MoeForCausalLM are not present in the repo
keyFacts.evidence: gh code search for 'Qwen3Next','Qwen3-Next','Qwen3-80B','Qwen3MoeForCausalLM' all returned zero items (main, 2026-06-12)

keyFacts.claim: tt_transformers only lists dense Qwen3 (Qwen3-32B, Qwen3-VL-32B); generic Qwen3-MoE lives in models/common/modules/moe configs (qwen3_235b.yaml etc.), not tt_transformers
keyFacts.evidence: model_config.py LOCAL_HF_PARAMS lists 'Qwen3-32B','Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct'; model_params/ has only Qwen3-32B & Qwen3-VL-32B; configs dir has qwen3_235b.yaml, qwen35_35b.yaml, qwen35_397b.yaml (num_routed_experts:128/256/512, num_shared_experts:0/1)

investigation.mechanism: No streaming/offload mechanism exists. The only host-side presence is the transient torch state_dict used during initialization, from which all expert weights are uploaded to device DRAM (tt_transformers Mixtral: ttnn.as_tensor with ShardTensorToMesh dim=0 + create_dram_sharded_mem_config; generic TTMoEDecode: ttnn.from_torch ShardTensorToMesh dim=1 then quantize to bfloat4_b on DRAM-sharded mem configs). MoE routing (all_to_all_dispatch_metadata / topk gate) only selects which already-resident on-device expert each token is sent to across the mesh; it never fetches weights from host per step. A cache_file_name option only persists the pre-formatted device tensor to disk to accelerate the next init. The single hook for path-based loading, _TTMoEDecodeExpertState._load_weights, is an unimplemented '# TODO ... support loading weights from path' / pass.

verification.summary: VERDICT: minor-corrections. The investigation's two load-bearing conclusions are CORRECT and fully supported by verbatim source: residentAllExperts = yes-all-resident, and dynamicLoadingAvailable = no. I re-read all cited tt-metal files via gh api raw and confirmed every quoted snippet exactly. Both MoE paths upload ALL expert weights to device DRAM at init and keep them resident: (1) tt_transformers Mixtral path (mixtral_mlp.py) concatenates all 8 experts and uploads via ttnn.as_tensor with ShardTensorToMesh(dim=0) into create_dram_sharded_mem_config (verified to return ttnn.BufferType.DRAM); forward() merely calls the resident expert module. (2) The generic models/common/modules/moe/tt_moe_decode.py uploads all routed experts via ttnn.from_torch ShardTensorToMesh(dim=1) then quantizes them resident to bfloat4_b on DRAM-sharded mem configs via quantize_weights_via_host; forward() feeds resident self.expert_state.tt_w0_w1/tt_w2 to ttnn.experimental.moe_compute, and routing (all_to_all_dispatch) only selects which already-resident on-device expert each token goes to — never a host weight fetch. The single path-based-loading hook, _TTMoEDecodeExpertState._load_weights, is a no-op '# TODO (AM) eventually support loading weights from path' / pass. No offload/stream/prefetch-from-host mechanism exists anywhere in either path. There is NOTHING analogous to llama.cpp --cpu-moe. ONLY CORRECTION (cosmetic, does not affect conclusions): the investigation claimed the 'A3B' code search returned 0 results; it actually returns 14, but all 14 are false-positive substring matches in unrelated files (kernel headers, SVGs, CI logs, READMEs), so the conclusion that Qwen3-Next-80B-A3B is unsupported in tt_transformers still holds (the genuinely-zero searches were Qwen3Next/Qwen3-Next/Qwen3-80B/Qwen3MoeForCausalLM). MEMORY-SIZING IMPLICATION CONFIRMED: count 100% of expert weights as device-resident; Mixtral shards total_expert_bytes/num_devices per device (8-chip mesh), generic module shards num_routed_experts across the mesh stored as bfloat4_b. No fraction of experts can be left on host at runtime. Key source files (all paths absolute within repo): models/tt_transformers/tt/mixtral_mlp.py, models/tt_transformers/tt/mixtral_moe.py, models/tt_transformers/tt/decoder.py, models/tt_transformers/tt/model_config.py (lines 3069/3105 detection, 3111-3119 DRAM mem config), models/common/modules/moe/tt_moe_decode.py (lines 74-75 TODO stub, ~360 _shard_experts, 507-524 bfloat4_b quantize, ~890 moe_compute).

investigation.area: tt-metal / tt-vllm MoE expert weight placement and weight-streaming/offload mechanisms (Angle 3)
investigation.mechanism: The only "streaming" is intra-device DRAM→L1/compute streaming (type i), NOT host→device (type ii). In the production DeepSeek-V3 demo, all routed experts are sharded and loaded into device DRAM at setup (memory_config=ttnn.DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG), with num_experts_per_device = n_routed_experts / num_devices. At runtime the MoE matmul (dram_streaming_experts_matmul) streams the selected expert's weight tiles from their DRAM bank shards into L1 over the NoC, indexed by the gate's top-k selection — the weights never leave device DRAM. A separate "SRAM hot expert" optimization (deepseek_v3_b1) pre-loads a small subset of frequently-routed experts into on-chip L1 SRAM slots to avoid the DRAM read, but this is a DRAM→L1 cache; the experts remain fully resident in DRAM. tt-metal's generic DRAM prefetcher (models/tt_transformers/tt/prefetcher.py, tt_metal/.../dram_core_prefetcher) is likewise a DRAM→L1 prefetch mechanism for dense models, not host offload. The HuggingFace-style cpu_offload references in tt-metal exist only in diffusion-model PyTorch reference/test code (e.g. stable_diffusion_xl_base), unrelated to TT device weight loading. The tt-vllm plugin (tenstorrent/vllm, plugins/vllm-tt-plugin) exposes NO cpu_offload_gb / offload / streaming knob: its TTModelLoader.load_model just calls model_class.initialize_vllm_model(...), delegating weight placement entirely to tt-metal (device DRAM), and check_and_update_config never references cpu_offload_gb. Consequence: required DEVICE DRAM cannot be made smaller than the full (block-float quantized) MoE model size by offloading experts to host. DeepSeek-V3 accordingly "requires a multi-host Galaxy setup" to fit; single-Galaxy only runs a reduced 5-layer subset, not the full model on less hardware.
investigation.findings:
## Summary

ALL MoE expert weights are loaded resident in device DRAM at startup. There is NO host-RAM→device dynamic/on-demand expert streaming in tt-metal or tt-vllm (nothing analogous to llama.cpp `--cpu-moe`). The only "streaming/prefetch" present is intra-device DRAM→L1, with experts still fully resident in DRAM. Required device DRAM therefore cannot be less than the full (block-float quantized) MoE model size.

## Evidence 1 — Experts loaded into device DRAM, sharded by device count (production DeepSeek-V3)

`models/demos/deepseek_v3/tt/experts.py`:
```python
assert hf_config.n_routed_experts % mesh_device.get_num_devices() == 0, (
    f"Number of experts ({hf_config.n_routed_experts}) must be divisible by the number of devices "
    f"({mesh_device.get_num_devices()})")
...
"input_tensor_b": shard_and_save(
    output_path / f"{ttnn_name}.input_tensor_b",
    cls._load_expert_weight(state_dict, hf_name, hf_config.n_routed_experts).unsqueeze(0).contiguous(),
    shard_dims=(1, 1),
    mesh_device=mesh_device,
    dtype=ttnn.bfloat8_b if hf_name == "down_proj" else ttnn.bfloat4_b,
    memory_config=ttnn.DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG,   # <-- experts placed in device DRAM
)
```
`_get_num_experts_per_device` returns `even_int_div(hf_config.n_routed_experts, mesh_device.get_num_devices())` and `_load_expert_weight` loops `for expert_id in range(n_routed_experts)` stacking ALL experts. So the entire expert set is distributed across the device mesh's DRAM; experts-per-device scales only with the number of physical devices, never with host RAM.

## Evidence 2 — Runtime is DRAM→L1 streaming (intra-device), not host streaming

`models/demos/deepseek_v3_b1/micro_ops/dram_streaming_experts_matmul/op.py` (docstring):
```
- Input B (in1): WIDTH_SHARDED in DRAM as [K, N], per-shard tiles reordered to column-major
... Each bank gets [K, per_core_N]
```
`models/demos/deepseek_v3_b1/unified_kernels/dram_streaming_experts_matmul.hpp`:
```
// Computes: output[1,N] = in0[1,K] @ in1[K,N] with in1 streamed from DRAM
//   NCRISC: Streams in1 from DRAM with pipelining (uses NOC_0), waits for output
```
`.../fused_ops/moe_routed_expert/op.py` (the MoE routed-expert flow):
```
5. Gate: top-K expert selection with normalized scores
6. Mcast expert indices to compute cores
7. DRAM streaming matmul + SiLU with indexed expert weights
```
The expert is selected by the gate, then its weight tiles are read from the expert's DRAM bank shard (`in1_tensor_addr` = DRAM address, indexed) into L1 over the on-chip NoC. Weights never leave device DRAM.

## Evidence 3 — "SRAM hot expert" is a DRAM→L1 cache, not host offload

`models/demos/deepseek_v3_b1/weights/transforms/sram_experts.py` (docstring) and `sram_slots.py`: a per-layer config "Maps layer_idx → list[expert_idx] specifying which routed experts to pre-load into L1 SRAM slots." `prepare_compressed_sram_slots` "stops at the first expert whose footprint would push any tracked core's lowest L1 address below boundary_addr." This trims how many hot experts fit in L1 (on-chip SRAM); the rest are served by DRAM streaming. The experts are still resident in DRAM — this reduces DRAM-read latency, not DRAM capacity.

## Evidence 4 — "upload" is one-time host→device staging at init, not runtime streaming

`models/demos/deepseek_v3_b1/weights/upload.py`: `get_fd_grid` -> "FD grid used for fast host-to-device transfers"; `_upload_tensors` "Upload host tensors by writing FD shards first" via `ttnn.allocate_tensor_on_device`. "host-staged" tensors are simply weights still in host RAM that get uploaded to device DRAM during model bring-up.

## Evidence 5 — tt-vllm has NO offload knob

`plugins/vllm-tt-plugin/src/vllm_tt_plugin/loader.py` (branch `dev`): `TTModelLoader.load_model` just calls `model_class.initialize_vllm_model(...)`; `download_model`/`load_weights` `raise NotImplementedError`. Grep of loader.py, worker.py, config.py, platform.py, model_runner.py, engine.py for `offload|cpu_offload|weight_stream|streaming|prefetch|on_demand|host_resident|mmap|paged.weight|expert.offload|resident|swap` returned ZERO matches. `platform.py::check_and_update_config` validates supported features (no distributed exec, no LoRA, prefix caching disabled if unsupported) and never references `cpu_offload_gb`. Standard vLLM `cpu_offload_gb` only acts inside the CUDA model loader, which the TT backend bypasses with its own TTModelLoader.

## Evidence 6 — capacity is bounded by hardware, not host RAM

`models/demos/deepseek_v3/README.md`: "DeepSeek-V3 requires a multi-host Galaxy setup. ... 2x or 4x configurations." Single Galaxy only runs "a reduced number of layers (5 layers instead of the full model)." You scale by adding devices, not by offloading experts to host.

## Note — `cpu_offload` hits in tt-metal are diffusion reference code only

The `cpu_offload` search hits (stable_diffusion_xl_base/tests/test_common.py, mochi, hunyuan) are HuggingFace Diffusers `enable_model_cpu_offload` on the PyTorch/CPU reference path — unrelated to TT device weight loading and not MoE.

## Conclusion for the VRAM calculator

For Tenstorrent MoE serving, model the required device DRAM as the FULL set of expert weights (in their block-float quantization, e.g. bfloat4_b for gate/up, bfloat8_b for down) plus non-expert weights and KV cache, divided across the device mesh. Do not assume any host-offload reduction of device DRAM.

keyFacts.claim: All routed experts are loaded into device DRAM at setup; experts-per-device = n_routed_experts / num_devices, must divide evenly
keyFacts.evidence: models/demos/deepseek_v3/tt/experts.py: 'assert hf_config.n_routed_experts % mesh_device.get_num_devices() == 0' and shard_and_save(..., memory_config=ttnn.DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG); _get_num_experts_per_device returns even_int_div(hf_config.n_routed_experts, mesh_device.get_num_devices())

keyFacts.claim: Runtime MoE matmul streams the selected expert's weights from DRAM bank shards into L1 over NoC (intra-device), not from host
keyFacts.evidence: dram_streaming_experts_matmul.hpp: 'Computes: output[1,N] = in0[1,K] @ in1[K,N] with in1 streamed from DRAM // NCRISC: Streams in1 from DRAM with pipelining (uses NOC_0)'; moe_routed_expert/op.py step 7: 'DRAM streaming matmul + SiLU with indexed expert weights'

keyFacts.claim: The 'SRAM hot expert' feature is a DRAM->L1 cache of frequently-routed experts, not a reduction of DRAM residency
keyFacts.evidence: weights/transforms/sram_experts.py: 'specifying which routed experts to pre-load into L1 SRAM slots'; sram_slots.py: trims at 'the first expert whose footprint would push any tracked core's lowest L1 address below boundary_addr'

keyFacts.claim: tt-vllm plugin exposes no offload/streaming/cpu_offload_gb; it delegates all weight placement to tt-metal device DRAM
keyFacts.evidence: plugins/vllm-tt-plugin/src/vllm_tt_plugin/loader.py: TTModelLoader.load_model calls model_class.initialize_vllm_model(...); grep of loader/worker/config/platform/model_runner/engine for offload|cpu_offload|streaming|prefetch|mmap|resident|swap returned zero matches; platform.py check_and_update_config never references cpu_offload_gb

keyFacts.claim: Required device DRAM cannot be reduced below the full quantized MoE model size; capacity scales by adding devices, not host offload
keyFacts.evidence: models/demos/deepseek_v3/README.md: 'DeepSeek-V3 requires a multi-host Galaxy setup ... 2x or 4x configurations'; single Galaxy runs only 'a reduced number of layers (5 layers instead of the full model)'

keyFacts.claim: The only cpu_offload references in tt-metal are HuggingFace Diffusers reference/test code, unrelated to TT device weight loading or MoE
keyFacts.evidence: models/demos/stable_diffusion_xl_base/tests/test_common.py comment: 'we do that before converting to dtype to avoid breaking in case we are using cpu_offload and half precision'

keyFacts.claim: tt-metal's DRAM prefetcher (dense models) is a DRAM->L1 prefetch, not host offload
keyFacts.evidence: models/tt_transformers/tt/prefetcher.py: 'Model configurations for which DRAM prefetcher is supported'; is_prefetcher_supported checks 'CB pages <= 65535, L1 size fits' (L1 capacity, on-chip)

keyFacts.claim: Weight 'upload' is one-time host->device staging at model bring-up, not runtime streaming
keyFacts.evidence: models/demos/deepseek_v3_b1/weights/upload.py: get_fd_grid 'FD grid used for fast host-to-device transfers'; _upload_tensors 'Upload host tensors by writing FD shards first' via ttnn.allocate_tensor_on_device

confirmed: residentAllExperts = yes-all-resident: CONFIRMED. models/demos/deepseek_v3/tt/experts.py places all routed experts in device DRAM at setup. Verbatim: '_convert_weights_default' calls 'shard_and_save(... cls._load_expert_weight(state_dict, hf_name, hf_config.n_routed_experts).unsqueeze(0).contiguous(), shard_dims=(1, 1), mesh_device=mesh_device, dtype=ttnn.bfloat8_b if hf_name == "down_proj" else ttnn.bfloat4_b, memory_config=ttnn.DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG)'. The function begins with 'assert hf_config.n_routed_experts % mesh_device.get_num_devices() == 0'.

experts-per-device = n_routed_experts / num_devices: CONFIRMED verbatim. '_get_num_experts_per_device' returns 'even_int_div(hf_config.n_routed_experts, mesh_device.get_num_devices())'. '_load_expert_weight' stacks ALL experts via 'for expert_id in range(n_routed_experts): ... expert_weights.append(...)' then 'torch.stack(expert_weights)'. Scales only with physical device count, never host RAM.

Quantization dtypes for VRAM modeling: CONFIRMED verbatim — 'dtype=ttnn.bfloat8_b if hf_name == "down_proj" else ttnn.bfloat4_b' (bfloat4_b for gate_proj/up_proj -> w1/w3, bfloat8_b for down_proj -> w2).

Runtime is intra-device DRAM->L1 streaming, NOT host->device: CONFIRMED. models/demos/deepseek_v3_b1/unified_kernels/dram_streaming_experts_matmul.hpp verbatim: '// Computes: output[1,N] = in0[1,K] @ in1[K,N] with in1 streamed from DRAM' and '// NCRISC: Streams in1 from DRAM with pipelining (uses NOC_0), waits for output'. micro_ops/.../op.py docstring: 'Input B (in1): WIDTH_SHARDED in DRAM as [K, N] ... Each bank gets [K, per_core_N]'. fused_ops/moe_routed_expert/op.py step 7: 'DRAM streaming matmul + SiLU with indexed expert weights'. Weights stay resident in DRAM.

SRAM hot expert is a DRAM->L1 cache, not host offload and not a DRAM-capacity reduction: CONFIRMED. weights/transforms/sram_experts.py docstring: 'Host-side preprocessing for SRAM hot expert weights ... turns one expert's float weight tensors into the host-side artifacts the SRAM hot expert orchestrator ... needs.' weights/sram_slots.py: 'prepare_compressed_sram_slots ... walks ... whose footprint would push any tracked core's lowest L1 address below ``boundary_addr``' and 'stops at the first expert whose predicted per-core footprint would push any tracked core's lowest occupied L1 address below ``boundary_addr``.' This trims how many hot experts fit in on-chip L1; the rest are served by DRAM streaming.

dynamicLoadingAvailable = no: CONFIRMED. tt-vllm plugins/vllm-tt-plugin/src/vllm_tt_plugin/loader.py verbatim: 'TTModelLoader.load_model' just calls 'model = model_class.initialize_vllm_model(model_config.hf_config, device_config.device, max_batch_size, ...)'; both 'def download_model' and 'def load_weights' bodies are 'raise NotImplementedError'. Grep of loader.py, platform.py, config.py, worker.py, model_runner.py for offload|cpu_offload|weight_stream|streaming|prefetch|on_demand|host_resident|mmap|swap|resident returned ZERO matches across all five files.

platform.py check_and_update_config never references cpu_offload_gb: CONFIRMED. Read of the full body (line 337+) shows it validates: chunked prefill disabled, no speculative decoding, TP/PP size == 1 ('TT backend does not support distributed execution'), no LoRA, clamps max_logprobs to 20, and registers TT model architectures. It contains zero references to cpu_offload / cpu_offload_gb / offload.

No host-offload mechanism exists in either repo: CONFIRMED. GitHub code search 'expert_offload repo:tenstorrent/tt-metal' returns total_count 0; 'cpu_offload_gb repo:tenstorrent/vllm' returns total_count 0. The only 'cpu_offload' hits in tt-metal are exactly three diffusion reference/test files (models/tt_dit/tests/models/mochi/test_pipeline_mochi.py, models/experimental/tt_symbiote/tests/test_hunyuan_video.py, models/demos/stable_diffusion_xl_base/tests/test_common.py) — HuggingFace Diffusers reference code, unrelated to TT device weight loading or MoE.

Weight 'upload' is one-time host->device staging at init, not runtime streaming: CONFIRMED. weights/upload.py: 'def get_fd_grid(device)' docstring 'Return the FD grid used for fast host-to-device transfers'; '_upload_tensors' docstring 'Upload host tensors by writing FD shards first, then SD-only shards' using 'ttnn.allocate_tensor_on_device(host_tensor.spec, device)'. Remaining tensors 'stay host-staged for the caller's' — staging at bring-up, not per-token streaming.

DRAM prefetcher (dense models) is DRAM->L1 prefetch, not host offload: CONFIRMED. models/tt_transformers/tt/prefetcher.py: 'is_prefetcher_supported' docstring 'Check if model can use DRAM prefetcher: CB pages <= 65535, L1 size fits, kv_heads % num_devices == 0' — all on-chip L1/CB capacity checks, no host involvement.

Capacity scales by adding devices, not host offload: CONFIRMED. models/demos/deepseek_v3/README.md: 'DeepSeek-V3 requires a multi-host Galaxy setup' with 'Galaxy (WH) - 2x or 4x configurations'; single Galaxy 'can also run ... with a reduced number of layers (5 layers instead of the full model)'.
verification.summary:
VERDICT: clean. Every load-bearing claim in the streaming-mechanisms investigation was independently re-verified against current tt-metal main and tt-vllm dev source via gh api, with verbatim matches. residentAllExperts=yes-all-resident and dynamicLoadingAvailable=no are both CORRECT.

Key confirmations: (1) models/demos/deepseek_v3/tt/experts.py loads ALL routed experts into device DRAM at setup via shard_and_save(..., memory_config=ttnn.DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG), with experts-per-device = even_int_div(n_routed_experts, num_devices) and a mandatory divisibility assert — residency scales with device count, never host RAM. Quantization is bfloat4_b for gate_proj/up_proj (w1/w3) and bfloat8_b for down_proj (w2). (2) The only "streaming" is intra-device DRAM->L1 over the NoC (dram_streaming_experts_matmul.hpp: 'in1 streamed from DRAM', 'NCRISC: Streams in1 from DRAM with pipelining'); weights never leave DRAM. (3) The "SRAM hot expert" feature (sram_experts.py / sram_slots.py boundary_addr trim) is a DRAM->L1 cache that reduces read latency, not DRAM capacity. (4) tt-vllm loader.py TTModelLoader.load_model only delegates to model_class.initialize_vllm_model(...); download_model/load_weights raise NotImplementedError; grep of loader/platform/config/worker/model_runner for offload|cpu_offload|streaming|prefetch|swap|resident = ZERO matches; platform.py check_and_update_config never references cpu_offload_gb. (5) GitHub code search: 'expert_offload' in tt-metal = 0 results, 'cpu_offload_gb' in tt-vllm = 0 results; the only cpu_offload hits in tt-metal are three HuggingFace Diffusers reference/test files (mochi, hunyuan, stable_diffusion_xl_base), unrelated to TT device weight loading.

There is NO host->device expert streaming/offload anywhere (nothing analogous to llama.cpp --cpu-moe). For the VRAM calculator: model required DEVICE DRAM as the full block-float-quantized MoE model (bfloat4_b w1/w3, bfloat8_b w2) plus non-expert weights and KV cache, divided across the device mesh. Do not assume any host-offload reduction; capacity scales only by adding devices (multi-host Galaxy 2x/4x for full DeepSeek-V3; single Galaxy runs only a 5-layer subset).

No corrections needed. Note: the plugin src dir on dev also contains engine.py (referenced by the investigation's grep list) plus async_decode.py, entrypoints.py, input_batch.py, launcher.py, model_registry.py, scheduler.py — all consistent with the no-offload finding.

synthesis.answer:
On Tenstorrent (tt-metal / tt-vllm), ALL MoE experts must be resident in device DRAM. There is NO dynamic, on-demand, or host-streamed expert loading — nothing analogous to llama.cpp `--cpu-moe`/`-ot`. This was triple-verified across the three production/generic MoE code paths and the vLLM plugin.

GPT-OSS (worked reference, models/demos/gpt_oss/tt/experts/weights.py): load_expert_weights places gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj and their biases via ttnn.as_tensor(device=mesh_device, memory_config=ttnn.DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG) at __init__. The torch tensors are reshaped to span the FULL (1, config.num_experts, ...) dimension — every expert goes to device DRAM. weight_dtype defaults to ttnn.bfloat4_b (4-bit, MXFP4-derived); biases ttnn.bfloat16. The decode/prefill forward path runs only ttnn.sparse_matmul over the already-resident weight fields with ZERO per-token host->device transfers (no from_torch/as_tensor/to_device/copy_host_to_device on weights). 120B (128 experts) shards EP x TP across the mesh (e.g. Galaxy mesh_4x8 => TP=8, EP=4 => 4 complete experts/device); the throughput path is genuine expert-parallel (num_experts/num_devices complete experts per device, HEIGHT_SHARDED in DRAM) and is disabled on Blackhole.

Same conclusion in every other path: tt_transformers Mixtral (mixtral_mlp.py concatenates all 8 experts -> ttnn.as_tensor ShardTensorToMesh(dim=0) into create_dram_sharded_mem_config = BufferType.DRAM); the generic models/common/modules/moe/tt_moe_decode.py (all routed experts -> ttnn.from_torch ShardTensorToMesh(dim=1), quantized resident to bfloat4_b on DRAM-sharded mem configs; its only path-loading hook _load_weights is an unimplemented '# TODO ... support loading weights from path' / pass); and DeepSeek-V3 (models/demos/deepseek_v3/tt/experts.py: shard_and_save(..., memory_config=ttnn.DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG) with experts-per-device = even_int_div(n_routed_experts, num_devices) and a mandatory divisibility assert).

The ONLY 'streaming' is intra-device DRAM->L1 over the NoC (dram_streaming_experts_matmul: 'in1 streamed from DRAM'); weights never leave DRAM. The 'SRAM hot expert' feature is a DRAM->L1 latency cache, not a DRAM-capacity reduction. The tt-vllm plugin (loader.py TTModelLoader.load_model just delegates to model_class.initialize_vllm_model(); download_model/load_weights raise NotImplementedError) exposes NO offload/cpu_offload_gb knob; platform.py check_and_update_config never references it. Capacity scales by ADDING DEVICES (multi-host Galaxy 2x/4x for full DeepSeek-V3; a single Galaxy only runs a reduced 5-layer subset), never by host offload. Large host min_ram_gb (300 GB for 120B) is a one-time load-time HF-checkpoint-conversion requirement, not host-resident experts during inference.

Bottom line for the calculator: model required device DRAM as 100% of expert weights resident, divided across the mesh. You cannot shrink it to 'active experts + working set'.
synthesis.recommendation:
NO STRUCTURAL FIX NEEDED. The core modeling choice — paramsTotal (all experts resident, sharded across chips, judged per-chip) — is CORRECT for Tenstorrent. There is no dynamic/host-streamed expert loading, so do NOT add any 'active-experts-only' or host-offload reduction; that would understate required device DRAM and is not supported by tt-metal/tt-vllm. Keep counting 100% of expert weights as device-resident, divided across the mesh (per-chip = total_expert_bytes / num_devices).

OPTIONAL REFINEMENT (accuracy, not correctness) on the bytes/param constant: the effective block-float bpp is not a single value because the on-device dtype mix differs per model. (1) GPT-OSS expert projections are all ttnn.bfloat4_b (4-bit) with ttnn.bfloat16 biases. (2) DeepSeek-V3 uses ttnn.bfloat4_b for gate_proj/up_proj but ttnn.bfloat8_b for down_proj (verbatim: `dtype=ttnn.bfloat8_b if hf_name == "down_proj" else ttnn.bfloat4_b`). bfloat4_b is ~0.5 B/param raw plus per-32-element shared exponent + tile padding; bfloat8_b is ~1.0625 B/param raw plus block-float overhead. A flat ~0.73 B/param is a fair blended estimate for a predominantly-bf4 model, but: a pure-bf4 MoE (e.g. GPT-OSS experts ignoring biases) trends LOWER than 0.73, while a model with substantial bf8 down-proj mass (DeepSeek-V3) trends HIGHER. If you want tighter sizing, compute expert bytes per-projection from each model's configured dtype (bf4 ~0.5-0.56, bf8 ~1.06-1.12 B/param incl. block-float scale/tile overhead) and add bf16 biases, rather than one global 0.73 constant. Also note non-expert weights (attention, router/gate, shared experts, norms, embeddings) and KV cache must be added on top and counted per-chip.
residualUncertainties: Exact effective bytes/param for block-float dtypes (bfloat4_b ~0.5-0.56, bfloat8_b ~1.06-1.12 B/param) depends on tile padding and the 32-element shared-exponent scale overhead; the flat ~0.73 B/param constant blends these but will be slightly off for pure-bf4 (lower) vs bf8-heavy (higher) models.
down_proj uses bfloat8_b in DeepSeek-V3 but bfloat4_b in GPT-OSS — a single global bpp constant cannot capture both; per-model/per-projection dtype handling would be more accurate.

Minor metric drift in tt-inference-server release_model_spec.json (gpt-oss-120b GALAXY now shows max_concurrency=1, data_parallel_size=4, not 32; spec now nested under model_specs[repo][DEVICE][engine][impl]) — does not affect the residency conclusion; min_ram_gb=300/min_disk_gb=260 (120B) and 50/60 (20B) confirmed verbatim.
These findings reflect tt-metal main and tt-vllm dev as of 2026-06-12; a future host-offload feature is conceivable but the only existing hook (_load_weights) is an explicit unimplemented TODO.
